summary.intent: User wanted to manage GitLab MR approval rules via CLI (adding/removing approval groups on a specific MR), then automate this process by adding `mr_approvers` configuration to ocp-build-data group.yml and updating the release_from_fbc pipeline to apply those rules after MR creation.
summary.outcome: Successfully used curl to add QE approval group and remove unwanted groups from MR #453. Implemented automation across three repos: added mr_approvers field to logging-6.5 group.yml, updated JSON schema validator, added set_mr_approval_rules method to GitLabClient, and hooked approval rule setup into release_from_fbc pipeline after MR creation.

repo: ocp-build-data group.yml is validated by JSON schema in art-tools with additionalProperties: false, requiring explicit property allowlisting
release_from_fbc.py uses doozer subprocess calls (config:read-group) to read group config, not direct file access
GitLab approval rules can be project-level (inherited) or MR-level (overrides); deleting at MR level removes override but keeps project-level rule
python-gitlab library already supports MR-level approval_rules.list()/create()/delete() natively

code.finding: New properties must be added to properties block before closing brace due to additionalProperties: false constraint
_load_product_from_group_config pattern shows how to use doozer subprocess to read group.yml fields via config:read-group
MR creation happens in create_shipment_mr; approval rule setup should be hooked immediately after mr.web_url is available

workflow: glab api has array parameter encoding issues with -f 'user_ids[]=123' syntax; curl with JSON body is more reliable
GitLab API DELETE returns 204 (no content) on successful approval rule deletion
Username to user ID resolution via GitLab API: users.list(username='xxx') or GET /users?username=xxx
Standard git workflow for art repos: fetch prod, branch from prod/main or prod/<version>, push to origin fork, PR to prod

friction: glab api failed to properly encode array parameters for user_ids, required fallback to curl
Initial DELETE requests appeared to succeed (204) but rules persisted when verified in sandbox due to network blocking
Project-level vs MR-level approval rule inheritance model was not immediately clear
open_items: Test the automation end-to-end with logging-6.5 after deploying code changes
Run unit tests for new GitLabClient.set_mr_approval_rules method and pipeline changes
Create and push branches for art-tools and ocp-build-data, raise PRs to prod
Roll out mr_approvers field to other logging-* branches after validating with logging-6.5
